title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in New Jersey
description: New Jersey voters will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the state’s Class II seat. The race matters because it will help determine control of the U.S. Senate in the 120th Congress, and New Jersey is generally a Democratic-leaning state that has recently elected Democrats to statewide federal office. The contest is currently centered on incumbent Cory Booker’s bid for reelection and a Republican primary field that includes several candidates with different political backgrounds and messaging.
polling_note: No public general-election polling for this race was found in the searched sources as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Cory Booker
candidates.summary: Cory Booker is the incumbent U.S. senator from New Jersey and is seeking reelection in 2026. He has built his political career around progressive themes including criminal justice reform, health care, civil rights, and economic opportunity, and he remains the best-known candidate in the race.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in New Jersey, 2026 - Ballotpedia
About Cory | U.S. Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey

candidates.name: Michael Misseri
candidates.summary: Michael Misseri is listed by Ballotpedia as the general-election candidate for U.S. Senate in New Jersey, though the page notes the candidate list is still unofficial and will be updated after the primary. Publicly available biographical information was limited in the searched sources, so his background and campaign profile could not be fully confirmed from the materials reviewed.

candidates.name: Chris Fields
candidates.summary: Chris Fields is listed by Ballotpedia as a Democratic primary candidate for U.S. Senate in New Jersey, and a campaign website describes him as a progressive Democrat focused on housing, health care, and practical reform. Beyond that campaign framing, the searched sources did not yield enough corroborated biographical detail to confirm a fuller career history.

candidates.name: Justin Murphy
candidates.summary: Justin Murphy is listed by Ballotpedia as a Republican primary candidate for U.S. Senate in New Jersey. His campaign site says he is running on themes such as education, the economy, the Second Amendment, and pro-life positions, and Ballotpedia notes he has served as deputy mayor of Tabernacle, New Jersey.
